description_long: This is a hand tool with a cylindrical handle that features alternating red and yellow panels, likely made of plastic for durability and grip. The handle is ergonomically contoured with subtle grooves for improved handling and comfort during use. Extending from the handle is a long, metallic shaft terminating in a Phillips head tip, designed for engaging cross-slotted screws. The shaft appears to be made of steel, providing strength and resistance to bending or twisting. The end of the handle is capped with a dark, possibly rubberized or plastic section, which may serve as a non-slip or impact-resistant feature. The overall construction suggests a robust and practical tool suitable for various urban maintenance or repair tasks.
description: A red and yellow contoured-handled screwdriver with a metallic Phillips head shaft, designed for comfortable grip and effective screwdriving.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Phillips tip and the full length of the shaft aligned with the handle's center.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical, contoured handle and the full length of the metallic shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the end cap of the handle and the symmetrical color pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the handle's contours and shaft alignment.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, straight, plastic handle with alternating red and yellow panels and a dark end cap for grip
